Applied_Species_Variety.reason: OsC2H2 knockout was tested in Nipponbare.

Protein_Family_or_Domain.reason: Figure 5 legend calls OsC2H2 a putative C2H2 zinc finger protein.

Regulator_Type.reason: It is one of the OsGBSSI-targeting TFs.

Subcellular_Localization.reason: No localization is given for OsC2H2.

Terminal_Metabolite.reason: The mutant shows reduced amylose content.

Terminal_Metabolite_Class.reason: Amylose is the starch polysaccharide measured.

Terminal_Metabolite_Function.reason: AC is explicitly stated to determine ECQ.

Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site.reason: The AC measured is in mature endosperm/grain.

Metabolic_Process_Controlled.reason: OsC2H2 is studied as an OsGBSSI-targeting TF affecting amylose biosynthesis.

Regulation_Mode.reason: Knockout lowers OsGBSSI expression/protein, supporting inferred positive regulation.

Primary_Regulatory_Targets.reason: OsC2H2 was selected as an OsGBSSI-targeting TF.

Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes.reason: The paper reports lower OsGBSSI transcript/protein in all mutants including osc2h2.

Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product.reason: It contributes to amylose tuning but with unfavorable appearance effect, as shown in the Results.

Core_Phenotypic_Effect.reason: The mutant lowers amylose and improves ECQ but significantly increases chalkiness.

Core_Validation_Method.reason: These methods are all used in the paper.

Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species.reason: The paper explicitly states OsC2H2 is highly expressed in developing endosperm.

Potential_Tradeoffs.reason: Higher chalkiness and slight reduction in 1000-grain weight are reported.

Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene.reason: These points are directly supported by the Results.

Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu.reason: The results support limited breeding utility because of increased chalkiness, though this exact phrasing is interpretive.
